title: Adi Badri temples
tagline: Adi Badri temples — spiritual town of Uttarakhand

overview.short: Sapta Badri constitutes a group of seven sacred Hindu temples, dedicated to god Vishnu, located in Garhwal Himalayas in the Indian state of Uttarakhand.
overview.description: Sapta Badri constitutes a group of seven sacred Hindu temples, dedicated to god Vishnu, located in Garhwal Himalayas in the Indian state of Uttarakhand. The Badrinath temple, called the Badri Vishal (altitude 3,133 m (10,279 ft)) is the primary temple among the seven shrines. The other six being Adi Badri, Bhavishya Badri, Yogadhayan Badri, Vriddha Badri, Ardha Badri and Dhyan Badri. The Panch Badri temple circuit consisted of only five temples, omitting Ardha Badri and usually Dhyan Badri (or sometimes Vriddha Badri).

topPlaces.name: Bhararisain
topPlaces.description: Bhararisain is the summer capital of the Indian state of Uttarakhand. It is located in Chamoli district's Gairsain tehsil, around 14 km from the town of Gairsain.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Adi Badri temples?
faq.a: The best time to visit Adi Badri temples is Mar – Jun, Sep – Nov. Summers are 15–28°C and winters are 2–16°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Adi Badri temples?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Dehradun Airport (Jolly Grant) (101 km) and the nearest railway station is Kathgodam (Nainital railhead) (103 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Adi Badri temples?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Adi Badri temples at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Adi Badri temples?
faq.a: Stays in Adi Badri temples start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.
